parameters.jsCode:
// Mycroft Agent 5 — Research Footprint Monitor
// Static watchlist of AI-sector companies tracked across two signals.
const watchlist = [
  { ticker: "NVDA",  company: "NVIDIA",
    wikipedia_title: "Nvidia",
    scholar_queries: ["NVIDIA", "NVIDIA Research"] },
  { ticker: "AMD",   company: "AMD",
    wikipedia_title: "Advanced_Micro_Devices",
    scholar_queries: ["AMD", "Advanced Micro Devices"] },
  { ticker: "INTC",  company: "Intel",
    wikipedia_title: "Intel",
    scholar_queries: ["Intel", "Intel Labs"] },
  { ticker: "GOOGL", company: "Google",
    wikipedia_title: "Google",
    scholar_queries: ["Google", "Google DeepMind", "Google Brain", "DeepMind"] },
  { ticker: "AAPL",  company: "Apple",
    wikipedia_title: "Apple_Inc.",
    scholar_queries: ["Apple Inc", "Apple Machine Learning"] },
  { ticker: "MSFT",  company: "Microsoft",
    wikipedia_title: "Microsoft",
    scholar_queries: ["Microsoft", "Microsoft Research"] },
  { ticker: "META",  company: "Meta",
    wikipedia_title: "Meta_Platforms",
    scholar_queries: ["Meta", "Meta AI", "FAIR", "Facebook AI Research"] }
];

return [{ json: { watchlist } }];

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({"model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile","temperature": 0.1,"max_tokens": 2000,"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are a research intelligence analyst for the AI investment sector. Analyze company research footprint data combining academic paper output and Wikipedia public knowledge activity. Return valid JSON only. No markdown, no preamble."},{"role": "user", "content": "Analyze the research footprint for these AI companies. For each company provide:\\n- research_narrative: 1-2 sentences summarizing their research presence and public mindshare\\n- academic_signal: LEADING | CONTRIBUTING | FOLLOWING | ABSENT\\n- wikipedia_signal: SURGING | ACTIVE | QUIET | DORMANT\\n- combined_insight: one sentence on what the combination of academic output and public knowledge activity says about this company\\n- watch_flag: true if either signal shows an unusual pattern worth monitoring\\n\\nReturn ONLY a JSON array:\\n[{\\n  \\\"ticker\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n  \\\"research_narrative\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n  \\\"academic_signal\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n  \\\"wikipedia_signal\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n  \\\"combined_insight\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n  \\\"watch_flag\\\": <boolean>\\n}]\\n\\nData:\\n" + JSON.stringify($json.groq_input)}]}) }}
